description: Default permissions for the desktop window. The structure: fs:scope allow=** (broad) with deny entries for sensitive locations (credentials, browser profiles, app's own session DB). Fs scope applies only to FRONTEND-INITIATED file operations via the FS plugin. Why broad: input files commonly live on D:/E:/ drives outside $HOME, and output files save next to the input — restricting to $HOME breaks multi-drive workflows. Why safe: the user picks files via native dialog (no path injection surface), and the load-bearing Rust backend commands enforce their own access controls independent of fs:scope: read_text_detect_encoding has an extension whitelist, subset_font checks the in-memory provenance cache, scan_font_directory canonicalizes + verifies starts_with the picked dir. Font directories are intentionally NOT in the deny list — Rust commands read fonts via std::fs::read after their own validation, not through the FS plugin. The deny list mirrors the cross-tool 'no app should ever read this' set + this app's own AppData/LocalAppData session DB. Permission bundles: dialog:default (tauri-plugin-dialog 2.7.0) already covers allow-message + allow-save + allow-open, so we do NOT list those individually — adding them would mislead a future reader into believing dialog:default is incomplete and proliferate explicit grants under that false assumption.
